settings.tagline: Visual stories told with intention — from the written word to the final frame. Cairo-based filmmaker crafting cinema that feels, breathes, and endures.
settings.tagline_ar: قصص بصرية بنية واضحة — من الكلمة المكتوبة إلى اللقطة الأخيرة.

settings.items: Scriptwriter,Film Production,Sound Design,Creative Direction,Visual Storytelling,Story Development,Cairo Egypt,قهوة الميتين

settings.label: About
settings.name: Omar Emad El Din
settings.stat_labels: Film Productions|Diplomas & Courses|Campaign Directed|Stories to Tell

settings.text: A filmmaking graduate from the British University in Egypt, Omar Emad El Din has spent years developing a voice rooted in authentic human experience — stories drawn from culture, memory, and the quiet tensions of everyday life.
From writing the graduation film Qahwet El Mayteen to directing brand campaigns and recording on documentary sets, Omar works at every layer of the filmmaking process with precision and deep passion.

settings.label: 02 — Films & Projects
settings.intro: Drag through a body of work spanning narrative short film, documentary production, and brand storytelling.

settings.title: Qahwet El Mayteen
settings.subtitle: The Coffee of the Dead
settings.logline: A grieving father keeps a nightly table at a Cairo coffeehouse for a son who stopped coming — and cannot bring himself to stop waiting.
settings.description: An original screenplay rooted in Egyptian folklore — a dark, claustrophobic narrative balancing tragedy with black humour, developed for the BUE graduation film.

settings.title: Documentary Film
settings.subtitle: Untitled Documentary Production
settings.logline: On-set audio capture across demanding locations — delivering broadcast-quality dialogue and ambient sound with real-time monitoring throughout production.
settings.description: Operated boom and field recording equipment, capturing broadcast-quality sound. Real-time level monitoring adapting to changing on-location acoustics.

settings.title: Placid Disruption
settings.subtitle: Placid Essentials — Brand Campaign
settings.logline: A scent brand reimagined as cinema — controlled stillness as the ultimate disruption.
settings.description: Conceptualized and directed the full creative vision for Placid Essentials flagship campaign — visual identity, storytelling concept, and tonal language from brief to final cut.

settings.title: The Script
settings.description: Every frame begins as a word. Character, conflict, and world are built on the page — where story logic, emotional truth, and visual possibility are tested before a single camera rolls.
settings.description_ar: كل لقطة تبدأ بكلمة. الشخصية والصراع والعالم تُبنى على الورق.

settings.title: Production
settings.description: On set, every department decision is a storytelling decision. From sound placement to actor blocking, the director's job is to translate the written word into something the camera can feel.
settings.description_ar: في موقع التصوير، كل قرار من أي قسم هو قرار سردي.

settings.title: The Cut
settings.description: The final story is found in the edit. Pacing, rhythm, and silence are the tools. What survives the cut is what the film actually says.
settings.description_ar: القصة الأخيرة تُكتشف في المونتاج. الإيقاع والصمت والتوقيت هي الأدوات.

settings.label: 11 — Script Analysis
settings.subtitle: Scene Analysis — Act I, Scene 1
settings.hint: Click a note or a highlighted line to expand.

settings.text: The room is almost empty. One table is lit. IBRAHIM, 60s, sits with the stillness of someone who has been here many times before. Two cups of coffee on the table. One untouched, perfectly placed.
A YOUNG WAITER watches from across the room.
Uncle Ibrahim... the kitchen is closing.
Ibrahim wraps both hands around his cup without looking up.
The waiter looks at the empty chair. Then at Ibrahim. He says nothing. He walks back.

settings.anchor: Two cups of coffee. One untouched, perfectly placed.
settings.note: The film's central tension is embedded in staging, not dialogue. Before a word is spoken, the audience understands: someone is absent but expected. The perfectly placed detail signals ritual — this is not the first night.
Three words carry the entire dramatic question: Is Ibrahim delusional? Loyal? In denial? The ambiguity is the engine. The film refuses to answer this until it must.

settings.anchor: He says nothing. He walks back.
settings.note: The waiter's silence is a structural choice, not absence of dialogue. A lesser draft had him offer condolences. This version trusts that what the waiter does not say is more powerful than any spoken comfort.
Official graduation short film screening, BUE Faculty of Arts & Sciences, Media & Mass Communication.
Full campaign launched across Placid Essentials brand platforms.

settings.label: 18 — Journal
settings.heading: Thoughts on
settings.sub: Writing on filmmaking, process, and the things that shape the work.

settings.title: On Writing the First Draft
settings.excerpt: The first draft is never about being good. It is about excavating something true from the noise.

settings.title: What the Documentary Taught Me
settings.excerpt: Three weeks on location with a documentary crew strips away every assumption you had about controlled filmmaking.

settings.title: On Directing Your First Actor
settings.excerpt: The director's job is not to direct performance. It is to create the conditions in which performance becomes inevitable.
